teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.

summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in process development, materials characterization, and failure/root-cause analysis across polymers, thin films, and functional molecular materials. Applies DOE/SPC, SOP/control-plan thinking, and multi-technique analytical readouts to stabilize high-variation R&D workflows and define reproducible process windows.

bullets: Lead DOE-guided bio-based polymer process development, defining process inputs/outputs, material-quality specifications, and reproducible operating windows for scale-up.
Assessed failure modes and structure-property-performance relationships across 15+ functional material variants using spectroscopy, electrochemistry, thermal analysis, chromatography, and MS.
Drove thin-film process development for AFOSR-funded coating systems, translating surface-preparation and CVD variables into controlled morphology and optical performance.
Developed reproducible CVD polymer thin-film workflows with DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring, connecting equipment conditions to growth kinetics and film quality.
Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented calibration, SOP, and safety systems that improved instrument uptime by 30%.

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Process
projects.description: Stabilized process variables and material-quality readouts for reproducible polymer scale-up using DOE/SPC and technical documentation.

projects.title: Electrochromic Material Reliability Study
projects.description: Mapped failure modes, degradation behavior, and performance across functional materials with orthogonal characterization.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, CV, TGA/DSC, NMR, GPC, MS

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Built controlled thin-film workflows by connecting CVD process variables to morphology, interfaces, and optical performance.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface prep, thin films

skills.Process Engineering: DOE, SPC, process windows, root cause analysis, control plans, SOPs, technical reports, reproducibility studies
skills.Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, ellipsometry, QCM-D, TGA, DSC, NMR, GPC/SEC, LC-MS, GC-MS, cyclic voltammetry
skills.Materials: Polymers, organic/organometallic materials, thin films, coatings, surface functionalization, structure-property-processing relationships
skills.Data & Statistics: JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel, structured experimental records, trend analysis, data integrity
skills.Operations: Lab operations, equipment tracking, calibration systems, safety/SOP systems, cross-functional resource planning
